## Abstract Create Demo

The `abstractCreateDemo` function helps you create structured demo factories that work seamlessly with the [`CodeHighlighter`](../../components/code-highlighter/page.mdx) component ecosystem. It provides a standardized way to create demos that combine component previews with code display, making it easier to build consistent documentation interfaces.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Overview
  - Factory Function Requirements
  - Implementation
  - Global Dependencies
    - Loader Configuration
  - Types
  - Related
- Exports:
  - abstractCreateDemo
    - Parameters: options, url, variants, meta
  - createDemoFactory
    - Parameters: options
  - createDemoWithVariantsFactory
    - Parameters: options

</details>

## Create Sitemap

The `createSitemap` factory function defines sitemap data for documentation sites. It works with the webpack loader for build-time precomputation in Next.js builds.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Overview
  - Runtime Loading
  - File Structure
  - Types
  - Configuration
    - Skip Precomputation
    - Webpack Loader Setup
  - Integration with Search
  - Populating the Sitemap
    - Customizing Index Entries
  - Related
- Exports:
  - createSitemap
    - Parameters: sourceUrl, pages, meta

</details>

6486048854,0.007237205255220125,-0.011400630795874169,0.030456448249784177]'

## Abstract Create Types

The `abstractCreateTypes` function helps you create structured type documentation factories that work seamlessly with the [`loadPrecomputedTypes`](../../pipeline/load-precomputed-types/page.mdx) loader. It provides a standardized way to create type documentation components that display TypeScript type information extracted at build time.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Overview
  - Implementation
  - Usage
    - Single Component
    - Multiple Components
    - Displaying Additional Types
    - Variant-Only Groups (Types-Only Modules)
    - Customizing Type Rendering
  - Enhancers
  - highlightAt
  - Build Integration
  - typeRefComponent
  - typePropRefComponent
  - linkProps
  - linkParams
  - linkScope
  - typeParamRefComponent
  - Factory Function Signatures
  - Advanced: Custom HOC Logic
    - Custom Factory with TypeScript Generics
  - Types
    - `abstractCreateTypes`
    - `createTypesFactory`
    - `createMultipleTypesFactory`
  - Additional Types
  - Related
- Exports:
  - abstractCreateTypes
    - Parameters: options, url, meta, exportName
  - createMultipleTypesFactory
    - Parameters: options
  - createTypesFactory
    - Parameters: options
- Types: AbstractCreateTypesOptions, ExportData, OrderingConfig, TypesTableMeta, defaultOrdering

</details>

## Abstract Create Demo Client

The `abstractCreateDemoClient` function helps you create client-side demo providers that manage external dependencies for live component demos. It creates provider components that supply externals context to child components, enabling dynamic code execution in the browser.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Overview
  - When to Use Demo Clients
  - Factory Function Requirements
  - Implementation
  - Usage Pattern
  - Client Provider Features
  - Externals Context
  - Best Practices
  - Example: Complete Live Demo Setup
  - Types
  - Related
- Exports:
  - abstractCreateDemoClient
    - Parameters: options, url, meta
  - createDemoClientFactory
    - Parameters: options
- Types: CreateDemoClientMeta

</details>

-0.05739163172482337,0.008849542631232218,0.0397516593566617,0.06266878746970843]'

[Read more](./abstract-create-demo-client/page.mdx)

## Create Demo Data

The `createDemoData` utility creates structured demo metadata and component data for use with the [`CodeHighlighter`](../../components/code-highlighter/page.mdx) system. It processes demo URLs, components, and metadata to create data objects that can be consumed by demo factories and code highlighting systems.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Features
  - Usage
    - Global Demo Data Structure
      - Global Variant Matching
  - When to Use
  - Types
  - Related
- Exports:
  - createDemoData
    - Parameters: url, component, meta
  - createDemoDataWithVariants
    - Parameters: url, variants, meta
  - createDemoGlobal
    - Parameters: url, globalProvider, meta
  - createDemoGlobalWithVariants
    - Parameters: url, globalProviders, meta
- Types: CreateDemoDataMeta, DemoData, DemoGlobalData, DemoGlobalProvider

</details>
